title: senegalia pennata subsp. insuavis
intro: Senegalia pennata subsp. insuavis, also known as the acacia tree or thorn acacia, is a species of plant in the Fabaceae family, characterized by its thorny branches and white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Senegalia pennata subsp. insuavis include wound healing, pain relief, and reducing fever, as well as potential protection against certain diseases and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's branches and leaves are typically harvested, dried, and used to make teas, infusions, or infusions-based remedies to leverage its medicinal properties.
